fromSplitwise.title: Leaving Splitwise? Welcome to HomePot
fromSplitwise.description: Welcome, Splitwise refugee. Zero ads, AI receipt scanning on the free tier, 52 currencies, and a transparent subscription model. Compare and try HomePot.
fromSplitwise.h1: Tired of ads and daily caps? Come on in.
fromSplitwise.intro: We built HomePot for the people who hit the 5-expense daily cap mid-trip, watched a cooldown timer before adding a €12 dinner, or gave up on receipt scanning because the total-only extraction was useless. You don't owe Splitwise your loyalty. Here's what we do differently.
fromSplitwise.props_subtitle: Practical differences you'll feel as soon as you start.

props.title: Zero ads — forever
props.desc: No banners, no interstitials, no cooldown timers. Our business model is an optional subscription, not your attention. Verified: there is not a single ad SDK in the HomePot codebase.

props.title: AI receipt scanner on the free tier
props.desc: Snap a photo of any receipt and our AI extracts the total, each item, taxes, tips, merchant and currency — not just the total. Available for free with a monthly scan limit; unlimited on Pro.

props.title: Recurring bills + monthly close
props.desc: Rent, internet, insurance — set them once and they repeat. At the end of the month, HomePot calculates the minimum transfers needed to zero everyone's balance, not a chain of small payments.

props.title: 52 currencies + AI reads any receipt
props.desc: 52 currencies selectable in the app, and the AI scanner detects any ISO 4217 currency directly from a receipt image. Auto-converts at today's exchange rate.

honest_note.title: HomePot isn't offline-first yet
honest_note.body: If offline expense entry is a hard requirement, HomePot currently needs a network connection (it syncs live). Tricount and the older Splitwise client both handle offline better right now. We're working on it, but we'd rather tell you upfront than let you find out on a plane.

faq.q: Can I import my Splitwise data?
faq.a: CSV import is on the roadmap. You can export your Splitwise group as CSV today and we'll ship an importer soon. Drop us a line at homepotapp@gmail.com and we'll notify you the day it lands.

faq.q: Is HomePot really free?
faq.a: Yes. The free plan includes 1 group, 20 expenses per month, an AI receipt scanner with a monthly scan limit, multi-currency, and zero ads — forever. Pro (from €2.92/month on annual billing) removes the limits and adds unlimited groups, exports and advanced analytics.

faq.q: How is this different from the Splitwise comparison page?
faq.a: This page is for people who already know they want to leave Splitwise. The full comparison is for people still deciding — it walks through every feature tier by tier with links to public sources.

faq.q: Are the claims about Splitwise honest?

tripSplitter.upsell_title: Want this automatic from your phone?
tripSplitter.upsell_desc: HomePot does the same math on your phone — plus AI receipt scanning, multi-currency, recurring bills and a monthly close ritual. Free tier is generous, Pro removes all the limits.

faq.q: Is this calculator really free?
faq.a: Yes. It runs entirely in your browser — we never see your data. No account, no email, no ads. If you want the same math automated on your phone across multiple trips, install HomePot.

faq.q: How does the settlement algorithm work?
faq.a: We compute each person's net balance (paid minus fair share) and then greedily match the largest creditor with the largest debtor until all balances are zero. This produces the minimum number of transfers needed to settle the group — not a chain of small payments between everyone.

faq.q: Can I share the result with my group?
faq.a: Share a screenshot for now. If you move to the HomePot app, every group member sees the same live numbers and the monthly close sends each person their exact transfer list.

faq.q: What currency does it use?
faq.a: It defaults to EUR and formats amounts using your browser locale. If your trip is in a different currency, the HomePot app handles 52 currencies natively plus any ISO 4217 currency detected by its AI receipt scanner.

roommates.title: How to split expenses with roommates fairly (2026 guide)
roommates.description: Practical guide to splitting rent, utilities and shared bills with roommates fairly. Covers equal splits, income-weighted splits, and common traps.
roommates.h1: How to split expenses with roommates fairly
roommates.intro: Sharing a flat is one of the most common ways people end up arguing about money. This guide walks through the three fair ways to split shared household bills, when each one actually makes sense, and how to handle the awkward cases (unequal rooms, different incomes, groceries no one tracks).

sections.h2: The three fair ways to split
paragraphs: When roommates share a flat, there are really only three mathematically "fair" ways to split shared expenses. Each one is fair under different assumptions, so it helps to know all three and pick the one that matches your household.
The wrong split isn't inherently unfair — it becomes unfair when the assumption behind it stops being true. The goal is to pick a model the whole group agrees with and then stop arguing.
list: Equal split. Everyone pays the same share. Works when everyone earns similarly, rooms are similarly sized, and consumption is similar.
Room-weighted split. Rent splits by room size or quality. Works when rooms differ significantly (master suite vs tiny box room).

Income-weighted split. Everyone pays the same percentage of their income, not the same absolute amount. Works when incomes differ significantly and the group wants equal financial burden, not equal cost.

sections.h2: Rent vs utilities vs groceries — they don't all split the same way
paragraphs: A lot of roommate conflict comes from applying one rule to every kind of expense. Rent, utilities and groceries behave differently and benefit from different rules.
Rent is usually best split using the model you agreed on when you moved in (equal, room-weighted, or income-weighted). Once set, don't renegotiate every month.

Utilities (electricity, gas, internet, water) usually split equally because consumption is hard to track per person and everyone benefits. The exception: if one roommate works from home every day, a small adjustment can be reasonable.

Groceries are the trickiest. Either keep them fully separate (everyone buys their own) or treat them as a shared pot where everyone contributes the same and takes what they need. Trying to split a grocery receipt item-by-item is where arguments start — save your energy.
sections.note: HomePot supports fixed and variable splits in the same group — rent splits one way, groceries split another, all automatically. You don't have to pick one model for everything.

paragraphs: If one roommate earns twice as much as another, splitting rent equally can leave the lower earner with a disproportionate share of their monthly income going to housing. This isn't unfair in an accounting sense, but it can be in a lived sense.

The fix is income-weighted split: add up everyone's net monthly income, compute what percentage of the total each person earns, and apply that percentage to the total rent. Everyone ends up paying the same share of their income on rent, which feels fair to most groups.
If that feels uncomfortable to discuss, start with equal splits and agree to revisit in three months. Low-friction is often more important than mathematically optimal.

sections.h2: Track shared expenses without a spreadsheet
paragraphs: Whatever split you agree on, tracking by memory or WhatsApp messages breaks down after a few weeks. The least-effort approach is an app that records each shared expense as it happens and computes the month-end balance automatically.

HomePot is built exactly for this: recurring rent and utilities, AI receipt scanning for the grocery trip nobody wants to log manually, and a monthly close that turns the balance into a short list of transfers. It's the lowest-friction way we've seen to stop arguing about who owes who at the end of the month.

upsell.title: Run your roommate finances on HomePot
upsell.desc: Recurring bills, fixed vs variable splits, AI receipt scanner on the free tier, and a monthly close that calculates the minimum transfers — all built for the household use case.

faq.q: Should we split everything equally?
faq.a: Only if everyone earns similarly and has similar-sized rooms. Otherwise, consider a room-weighted split for rent and an income-weighted split for everything else. The key is that the whole household agrees on the rule before applying it.

faq.q: What if one roommate always buys the groceries?
faq.a: Have them track the grocery receipts as shared expenses and at month end everyone pays their share. HomePot's AI receipt scanner can read the total plus line items, which lets you exclude anything that wasn't shared (e.g., a specific brand one roommate buys).

faq.q: How do we handle a roommate who moves out mid-month?
faq.a: Pro-rate rent and utilities for the days they were there. Most rental apps (and HomePot) let you add a dated end to a shared expense so the monthly close handles the math correctly.

faq.q: Is it worth tracking small expenses like toilet paper?
faq.a: Usually not. Tracking every €3 purchase creates more friction than it solves. A common approach is a monthly "household pot" — everyone puts in a fixed amount that covers shared small items and nobody has to track them individually.

tricount.title: HomePot vs Tricount — honest comparison (2026)
tricount.description: Tricount is the gold standard for free trip-splitting. HomePot is built for ongoing household finances with AI receipt scanning. Honest side-by-side with sources.
tricount.h1: HomePot vs Tricount — the honest comparison
tricount.intro: Tricount (by bunq) is probably the best-rated free expense-splitting app on iOS, and it genuinely deserves it: simple, 100% free, great for one-off group trips. HomePot plays a different game — ongoing household finance with AI receipt scanning, recurring expenses, fixed vs variable splits, and a monthly close ritual. Both are honest, ad-free apps; this page shows exactly where each one wins so you can pick the right tool.
tricount.table_subtitle: Each cell states how the feature is offered in each app. Sources linked where possible.

features.label: AI receipt scanner with OCR
features.ours: Free (monthly scan limit) · Unlimited on Pro

features.label: Fixed vs variable split modes in the same group
features.theirs: Split equally, by parts, or custom amounts

features.label: Groups
features.ours: 1 free household group · Unlimited on Pro

differentiators.title: AI reads every line of the receipt
differentiators.desc: HomePot's scanner doesn't just attach the photo — it extracts the total, each line item, taxes, discounts, tips, merchant and currency, so you can split a restaurant bill exactly the way the group consumed it. Tricount lets you attach a receipt photo, but you still enter the amount and items by hand.

differentiators.title: Built for recurring household finance
differentiators.desc: Rent, internet, insurance, utilities — HomePot has first-class recurring expenses, fixed vs variable split modes in the same group, and a monthly close with minimum-transfer settlement. Tricount is designed around one-off trips and continuous settlement, and doesn't list recurring expenses as a feature.

differentiators.title: 52 currencies + AI reads any receipt
differentiators.desc: Both apps handle multi-currency. HomePot ships 52 currencies in the expense picker and, thanks to the AI scanner, can read any ISO 4217 currency directly from a receipt image and auto-convert at live exchange rates, even if it's outside those 52.

differentiators.title: Ongoing balances & history — not just per-trip
differentiators.desc: HomePot is designed for households that want to track shared finance month after month, with historical stats per category and a ritual monthly close. Tricount shines for a single trip where you want to settle once and move on.

honest_note.title: When Tricount is actually the better choice
honest_note.body: If your only need is splitting a one-off group trip — a weekend away, a ski trip, a wedding — Tricount is outstanding. It's free with no paid tier, genuinely offline-capable, has Splitwise import built in, and sits at 4.9 / 5 on the iOS App Store (5,497 ratings at time of writing). We'd honestly recommend it for that use case. HomePot is built for a different job: ongoing household and roommate finances where recurring bills, AI receipt scanning, fixed vs variable splits, and a monthly close ritual actually matter.

faq.q: Is Tricount really 100% free?
faq.a: According to Tricount's own website and feature page, yes — Tricount is described as "100% free" with no paid subscription tier advertised. Features like multi-currency, offline mode, Splitwise import and spending insights are all listed as free on tricount.com. HomePot has a free tier too, plus an optional Pro subscription that removes limits on groups, expenses and AI scans.

faq.q: Does Tricount have AI receipt scanning?
faq.a: Tricount's official feature page mentions attaching photos of receipts to expenses, but does not list OCR or AI extraction as a feature. HomePot's AI scanner is built on Claude Haiku and extracts the total, line items, taxes, merchant and currency directly from the receipt image.

faq.q: Can I import my Tricount data into HomePot?
faq.a: Not yet. Tricount can import from Splitwise; HomePot's own importer is on the roadmap. Write to homepotapp@gmail.com and we'll notify you the day it ships.

faq.q: Which app should I choose for a one-off group trip?
faq.a: For a single group trip where you just want to split expenses and settle once, Tricount is an excellent and honest choice — it's free, offline-capable and extremely well-reviewed. HomePot is a better fit if the group shares expenses month after month, wants AI receipt scanning, recurring bills, fixed vs variable splits and a monthly close ritual.

faq.q: Are the facts about Tricount on this page up to date?
faq.a: All Tricount claims on this page come from Tricount's official website (tricount.com and tricount.com/en-us/expense-tracker-features) and its iOS App Store listing at the time of writing. Please verify current details on those sources since they can change. HomePot is not affiliated with Tricount or bunq, and the comparison is our own opinion based on publicly available information.

splitwise.h1: HomePot vs Splitwise — the honest comparison
splitwise.intro: Splitwise has been the default way to split shared expenses for over a decade. Both HomePot and Splitwise have a free tier and a paid Pro tier, so a fair comparison must show what each plan actually gives you. Here's the honest breakdown — and the short version is that most of what Splitwise reserves for Pro, HomePot includes for free. Every row below links to a public source so you can verify it yourself.
splitwise.table_subtitle: Each cell states which plan tier the feature is available on. A checkmark means unrestricted for all users; text means there's a specific tier or limit.

features.ours: Free (monthly scan limit) · Unlimited on Pro

AI reads any currency from the receipt image
Expenses per month

features.theirs: Daily cap applies — see Splitwise Pro page

features.ours: 1 free household group · Unlimited on Pro

differentiators.title: AI reads every line of the receipt
differentiators.desc: Our scanner doesn't stop at the total. It extracts each item, tax, discount, and tip so you can split a restaurant bill exactly the way the group consumed it — not by a forced even share.

differentiators.title: 52 currencies + AI reads any receipt
differentiators.desc: 52 currencies supported for expense entry, covering Europe, the Americas, Asia, the Middle East, Oceania and Africa. The AI scanner goes further and detects any ISO 4217 currency from the receipt image, then auto-converts to your group's base currency at live exchange rates.

differentiators.title: Zero ads, zero trackers — forever
differentiators.desc: We make money from optional subscriptions, not from your attention or your data. No interstitial ads, no cooldown timers, no third-party analytics SDKs. This is a written, verifiable policy — not a marketing tagline.

differentiators.title: Fixed vs variable split in the same group
differentiators.desc: Rent, internet and insurance split evenly between everyone. Dinner and groceries split only between the people who were there. HomePot models both cases natively in the same group — no workarounds, no separate spreadsheets.

differentiators.title: Monthly close with minimum transfers
differentiators.desc: At the end of the month, HomePot computes the smallest set of transfers needed to bring every balance back to zero. Instead of chains of small payments between many people, you get one or two clean settlements and the month is closed.

differentiators.title: Real privacy, documented in plain language
differentiators.desc: A public GDPR-compliant policy, data hosted on AWS EU, one-tap account and data deletion from Settings. Nothing hidden in fine print and nothing sold to data brokers.

faq.q: Can I import my Splitwise data into HomePot?
faq.a: CSV import is on our roadmap. In the meantime you can export your Splitwise group as CSV and a HomePot importer will land soon. Write to homepotapp@gmail.com and we'll notify you the day it ships.

faq.q: Is HomePot really free?
faq.a: Yes. The free plan includes 1 group, 20 expenses per month, 10 AI receipt scans, multi-currency, and zero ads — forever. Pro starts at €2.92/month on annual billing and removes the limits, plus adds unlimited groups, exports and advanced analytics.

faq.q: What exactly do I get on HomePot's free tier, without paying?
faq.a: A free HomePot account gives you: 1 group with unlimited members, 20 expenses per month, a limited number of AI receipt scans per month, receipt photo uploads, the full 52-currency picker, charts and spending analytics, recurring expenses, fixed and variable splitting in the same group, the monthly close with minimum-transfer settlement, dark mode, and zero ads. Pro mainly removes the group/expense/scan limits and adds CSV/PDF export for your stats.

faq.q: How does HomePot make money if there are no ads?
faq.a: Optional subscriptions. Pro is €2.92/month on annual billing — less than a coffee per month. That's the only revenue model. No ads, no data selling, no affiliate placements inside the app.

faq.q: How many currencies does HomePot really support?
faq.a: 52 currencies are selectable from the picker for expense entry, covering Europe, the Americas, Asia, the Middle East, Oceania and Africa. On top of that, the AI receipt scanner detects any ISO 4217 currency directly from the receipt image — so even if your group's base currency is outside the 52, you can still scan a receipt anywhere in the world and see it auto-converted.

faq.q: Is HomePot GDPR compliant?
faq.a: Yes. We comply with GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), and LGPD (Brazil). Data is hosted on AWS in the EU, encrypted at rest and in transit. Complete account and data deletion is available in one tap from the Settings screen.

faq.q: Are the facts about Splitwise on this page up to date?
faq.a: All Splitwise claims on this page are taken from their public website (splitwise.com/pro) and the Splitwise iOS App Store listing at the time of writing. Please verify current pricing and features on those sources, since they can change. HomePot is not affiliated with Splitwise, Inc. and the comparison is our own opinion based on publicly available information.
